raw_observation_points: The main issue is unclear review target: the user could not tell what should be judged from this video.
If the target is cut quality, the video appears cut too tightly at both ends, even if it aligns with subtitle timing.
The subtitle direction differs from what would be useful for a clipping/cutout video, so the user does not know how to evaluate it.
Audio is original source audio, so it is unlikely to be broken and is not a meaningful evaluation axis here.
The subtitle is not broken, but it looks almost the same as ordinary YouTube subtitles.
VLC screenshot context shows the ED-10au micro-scene with a large black-box Japanese subtitle near the bottom; screenshot context alone does not prove layout failure.

observation_classifications.basis: The user opened the recovered ED-10au MP4 directly through the command.
The recovered local MP4 was available and opened on the Thank terminal.
The user did not report broken subtitle timing or broken subtitle rendering.
Audio is reused original source audio and was not observed as broken.
The user could not tell what should be judged from the video.
observation_classifications.approval_implication: review target is not clear enough for acceptance

observation_classifications.basis: If judging cut quality, both ends appear too tight even though the clip aligns with subtitle timing.
observation_classifications.approval_implication: v2 route is justified, not approval

observation_classifications.basis: The subtitle direction looks close to ordinary YouTube subtitles and does not help the user evaluate clipping/cutout usefulness.
Screenshot context shows a large black-box subtitle near the bottom, but screenshot context alone does not prove rendered layout failure.
The observation describes review target, cut-window, and subtitle-strategy mismatch rather than a concrete render-path gap.
The observation is a review readback only.
The user identified review-target and v2-route issues.

route_decision.enabled_reason: The user has now confirmed source/cut-window/review-target mismatch: the current specimen is openable, but both ends feel too tight if judged as cut quality, and the subtitle strategy does not help clipping/cutout review.

route_order.use_when: Design a v2 specimen that lets the reviewer judge cut-window and clipping-review usefulness rather than only subtitle burn-in.
Use only if the later v2/design pass needs to separate rendered subtitle placement from VLC/player UI or black-box subtitle presentation.
Use only if a concrete render-path gap appears; the current observation does not identify one.
Use only if audio is later observed broken; the current audio is source reuse and not a meaningful failure axis.
Use only if v2 cannot be designed from the current source and observation.

primary_rejected_routes.subtitle_layout_screenshot_capture: not primary because screenshot context alone does not prove layout failure
primary_rejected_routes.final_render_path_stage_4: not primary because no concrete render-path gap was observed
primary_rejected_routes.timing_audio_check: not primary because original source audio was reused and not observed broken
primary_rejected_routes.another_pure_review_frame_packet: not primary because ED-10ax already clarified the frame and the user now supplied enough mismatch evidence for v2 design

v2_design_constraints.purpose: Make the next specimen evaluate cut-window and clipping-review usefulness, not only subtitle burn-in.
v2_design_constraints.audio_policy: reuse original source audio; do not make audio the first failure axis unless later observed broken
v2_design_constraints.media_tracking_policy: if v2 is rendered later, keep MP4/ASS/manifest under ignored episodes paths and track no media
v2_design_constraints.approval_policy: v2 must not claim production, public, rights, publishing, or monetization approval

next_v2_implementation_route.objective: Build a bounded ED-10ba or successor v2 internal-review specimen using a slightly less tight source window and a review-purpose-aligned subtitle treatment.
execution_constraints: start from the same scene intent unless transcript/source review shows a better nearby exchange
add modest pre-roll and post-roll handles when source timing allows
make the review purpose explicit as cut-window and clipping-review usefulness
